title: Mixed Feeding for Dogs & Cats: How to Combine Wet and Dry Food the Right Way
content:
If you’ve ever stood in the pet food aisle or browsed through PetStore Kenya products, you’ve probably asked yourself: “Should I feed my pet wet food, dry food, or both?” But what if you don’t want to choose? What if you could give your pet the best of both worlds?

Mixed feeding, which combines wet and dry food, is one of the best ways to ensure your dog or cat enjoys balanced nutrition, variety, and hydration. It’s becoming more popular among pet parents, and for good reason. But it also raises a lot of questions. Am I doing it right? Is it healthy for them? How much should I feed them?

At PetStore Kenya, we’re here to help you master mixed feeding for dogs and cats. This comprehensive guide will take out the guesswork, giving you the knowledge and confidence to make mealtime a breeze for both you and your fur-ever friend.

What Exactly Is Mixed Feeding?

Simply put, mixed feeding (also known as “combination feeding“) is giving your pet both dry and wet food as part of their daily diet. This isn’t just about adding a spoonful of wet food to their kibble as a treat; it’s about creating a balanced meal plan where each type of food provides a specific portion of their daily nutritional needs.

 

There’s no single right way to mix feed; it’s about finding what works for you and your pet. For instance, you might feed a portion of dry food in the morning and a portion of wet food in the evening, mix a little bit of wet and dry food in the same bowl, or even alternate between full days of kibble and full days of wet food. The key is to ensure the total daily amount provides all the calories and nutrients your pet needs, without overfeeding.

Why Is Mixed Feeding So Beneficial?

Mixed feeding for dogs and cats is not just about variety; it’s about providing a strategic blend of benefits that can improve your pet’s overall health and happiness.

         1. Hydration Support

When it comes to hydration, wet food is a true game-changer, especially for cats. With 70–85% moisture (compared to just 10% in dry food), it naturally boosts your pet’s water intake, which is essential for urinary tract health and kidney function.

While we might see our dogs lapping up water from their bowls, cats are a different story. Cats evolved from desert-dwelling ancestors and are naturally programmed to get most of their moisture from the prey they eat. Since dry kibble contains very little water, many cats live in a constant state of mild dehydration, which can put a long-term strain on their kidneys and urinary system. Even for dogs, adding wet food ensures they are consistently getting adequate water, which is crucial for overall health, especially for active dogs or those living in warm climates. Think of it as an extra layer of protection for their most vital organs.

By incorporating wet food, you’re not just adding a tasty treat; you’re actively supporting your pet’s internal health. The high moisture content helps:

  • Promote Kidney Health: Proper hydration allows the kidneys to function efficiently, flushing out waste products and toxins. For pets, especially cats, this efficient flushing is a major factor in preventing chronic kidney disease, a common ailment in older animals.
  • Prevent Urinary Tract Issues: Diluted urine is less likely to form crystals and stones, which can lead to painful and dangerous blockages. This is a critical benefit for pets prone to urinary tract infections (UTIs) or feline lower urinary tract disease (FLUTD).
  • Aid Digestion: Water helps move food through the digestive tract, preventing constipation and promoting regular, healthy bowel movements.
         2. The Palatability Factor

If you have a picky eater, you know the struggle is real. The rich aroma and tender texture of wet food can be irresistible, instantly making mealtime more appealing. Unlike dry kibble, which has a more subtle scent, wet food’s strong aroma acts as a powerful motivator, encouraging even the most finicky of pets to eat.

Adding just a small portion of wet food to their dry kibble can transform a boring meal into an exciting event. This is particularly helpful for pets who are ill, have a reduced appetite, or are simply tired of the same old kibble. The moisture and soft texture of wet food also make it easier to chew, which is great for older pets or those with dental issues. By making mealtime more enjoyable, you ensure they get the nutrition they need to stay healthy and happy.

        3. Nutritional Variety

While a single type of food can be a complete diet, different brands and formulas offer unique nutrient profiles that can benefit your pet. By combining wet and dry foods, you’re not just providing a complete meal, but you’re also introducing a broader spectrum of nutrients, vitamins, and minerals.

For example, some dry foods are rich in carbohydrates to provide energy and have a dense, crunchy texture that helps with dental health. On the other hand, wet foods often contain higher levels of protein and fats, which can be essential for muscle maintenance and coat health. By mixing, you create a synergy where the strengths of one food complement the other.

This can be especially useful for pets with specific dietary needs or those who are aging and may benefit from a combination of different nutrient sources. It’s like creating a well-rounded meal for yourself, rather than eating the same single item every day.

        4. Dental Health

Dental disease is one of the most common health problems in adult dogs and cats, but it’s often overlooked because many pet parents aren’t aware of the early signs, such as bad breath, and pets are masters at hiding pain. The disease is so prevalent because plaque and tartar build up on pets’ teeth just like they do on ours, and since they can’t brush on their own, the problem gets worse over time.

Dry kibble plays an important role in helping to prevent this. As your pet chews on the hard, abrasive kibble, it acts like a natural toothbrush, physically scraping away plaque and tartar from the surface of their teeth. This simple, daily action helps to reduce the buildup that can lead to gingivitis, tooth decay, and painful infections. By keeping a portion of dry kibble in their diet, you’re giving your pet an easy way to maintain dental hygiene and support their overall health for years to come.

         5. Weight Management

For pets on a diet, managing hunger is often the biggest challenge. This is where the power of mixed feeding for dogs and cats truly shines. Wet food is significantly less calorie-dense than dry kibble because of its high moisture content (up to 85% water). This means you can serve a larger volume of food to help your pet feel full and satisfied without overfeeding them on calories.

Think of it this way: when you swap some dry food for wet food, you make your pet’s meal look much bigger. This larger meal, plus the extra protein in the wet food, makes your pet feel full in their belly. This simple trick is the best way to stop them from being hungry and to cut down on them begging for snacks between meals.

The Stress-Free Guide to Mixing Wet and Dry Food for Your Pet

Ready to give it a try? Here’s your step-by-step guide to a successful transition with mixed feeding for dogs and cats:

Step 1: Calculate the Total Daily Calories

This is the most important part. You cannot simply feed a full portion of wet food and a full portion of dry food; that’s a recipe for weight gain.

  • First, determine your pet’s daily calorie needs based on their weight, age, and activity level. You can find this information on the back of your food package.
  • Next, decide on the ratio you want to use. A common starting point is a 50/50 split (50% from dry food, 50% from wet food).
  • Use the feeding guidelines on both bags to calculate the exact amount of each to give. For example, if your dog needs 300g of dry food per day, a 50/50 split means you’ll feed 150g of dry food and a proportional amount of wet food that provides the same number of calories as 150g of dry food.

Step 2: Start Slowly

Just like with any dietary change, a gradual transition is key to preventing an upset stomach. Over a period of 7 days, slowly increase the amount of the new food (wet food) while decreasing the amount of the old food (dry food).

  • Days 1-2: 75% old food, 25% new food.
  • Days 3-4: 50% old food, 50% new food.
  • Days 5-6: 25% old food, 75% new food.
  • Day 7: 100% new meal plan.
Step 3: Serve It Right

You have a few options for how to serve the mixed meal:

  • Mixed in the Bowl: Combine the wet and dry food directly in the bowl. This is great for picky eaters, as the wet food’s aroma and flavour will coat the kibble.
  • Separate Bowls: Place the wet food in one bowl and the dry food in another, allowing your pet to choose. However, please note that wet food should not be left out for long periods. After about 30 minutes, it can start to spoil and attract bacteria. It’s best to only put out what your pet will eat in one sitting, or to manage the feeding times so the food doesn’t sit for an extended time.
  • Separate Meals: Feed dry food in the morning and wet food in the evening.

Mistakes to Avoid

  • Don’t eyeball portions. Use a proper measuring cup or a scale to be precise with both food types.
  • Don’t ignore the calorie count. A half-and-half split by volume is not the same as a half-and-half split by calories. Always check the labels.
  • Don’t forget to clean the bowl. Wet food can leave a residue that can lead to bacterial growth. Wash the bowl after every meal!

Final Thoughts: The Best of Both Worlds for Your Pet

There’s no one-size-fits-all answer when it comes to feeding, but mixed feeding for dogs and cats is an excellent middle ground. Whether you’re feeding a picky cat or a playful pup, a thoughtful mix of wet and dry food adds variety, hydration, and balanced nutrition, turning every mealtime into something your pet looks forward to. Start small, pay attention to your pet’s cues, and let their response guide you. Over time, you’ll discover the balance that truly works for your pet’s needs.

Now that you’ve got the basics of mixed feeding down, it’s all about picking the foods that work best for your furry friend. Ready to give them the best of both worlds? At PetStore Kenya, you’ll find a wide selection of wet and dry food from trusted brands like Reflex, Miglior and Spectrum available online, in-store, and at select neighbourhood supermarkets. Explore our food collections today and start your mixed feeding journey with confidence!

👉 Browse our pet food selection here

📍 Visit us in-store at one of our convenient locations for personalized advice

💬 Chat with our team online via WhatsApp for quick questions and tips. You can also join our WhatsApp Channel for more tips and updates.

Remember, a well-nourished pet is a happy pet, and a happy pet makes for a happy home. We are here to support you every step of the way in caring for pets who thrive, every single day!

The PetStore Kenya Team

title: 🥣 Wet vs. Dry Pet Food: Which Is the Best Food for Your Dog & Cat?
content:
As a loving pet parent, you want nothing but the best for your furry family member. Their health and happiness often begin right in their food bowl because the food they eat provides all the essential building blocks, energy, and nutrients their bodies need to thrive. Just like humans, a balanced diet is the foundation for strong bones, healthy organs, a shiny coat, boundless energy, and a strong immune system to fight off illness. But step into any pet store, and you’re immediately faced with a dizzying array of choices: wet food, dry kibble, low grain, senior formulas, breed-specific diets… it’s enough to make anyone’s head spin!

Choosing the right food can feel overwhelming, and it’s a decision with real consequences. Giving your pet the wrong type of food, or a diet that doesn’t meet their specific needs, can unfortunately lead to various issues. You might start noticing things like an upset stomach, frequent diarrhoea, or vomiting. Their beautiful coat could become dull and flaky, or they might suffer from constant itching. Over time, an unsuitable diet can lead to more serious problems like unhealthy weight gain or loss, low energy levels, and even long-term health complications affecting their organs or overall vitality. It’s a bit like us trying to live on fast food alone – it might fill us up, but it won’t keep us healthy in the long run!

One of the most common questions we hear at PetStore Kenya, especially from new pet parents, is: “Should I feed my dog or cat wet food or dry food?”

The honest truth is, there’s no single “right” answer that fits every pet. Just like humans, every dog and cat is a unique individual with their own needs, preferences, and health considerations. What works wonderfully for one might not be ideal for another.

The honest truth is, there’s no single “right” answer that fits every pet. Just like humans, every dog and cat is a unique individual with their own needs, preferences, and health considerations. What works wonderfully for one might not be ideal for another.

But don’t worry, you don’t have to navigate this journey alone! This comprehensive guide is designed to demystify the world of pet food, helping you understand the ins and outs of both wet and dry options. Whether you’re a seasoned pet owner or just welcoming your first four-legged companion, we’ll break down the benefits and considerations of each, so you can make an informed decision that truly nourishes your beloved pet!

Understanding the Basics: What’s in Your Pet’s Bowl?

Before we dive into the specifics, let’s clarify what we mean by “wet food” and “dry food.”

What is Wet Food?

Imagine opening a can or pouch of pet food, and you see soft, moist chunks, pâtés, or shredded meat in gravy. That’s wet food! It’s also commonly known as canned or pouched food. The defining characteristic of wet food is its high moisture content, typically ranging between 70–85%. This is significantly higher than dry kibble, making it feel more like a fresh meal.

Wet food comes in various textures, offering different experiences for your pet:

  • Chunks (often in gravy or jelly): These feature distinct pieces of meat, fish, chicken or any other animal protein, often suspended in a savoury gravy or a gelatinous jelly. They provide a satisfying, textured meal.
  • Pâté: This type has a smooth, consistent, and often dense texture, similar to a finely ground meatloaf. It’s easy for pets to lick and can be very appealing.
  • Mousse: A very light, airy, and whipped texture, mousse is often highly palatable and easy to consume, especially for very young, old, or recovering pets.

Curious to see these textures up close? We’ve put together a short video that visually demonstrates the different types of wet food textures we offer – from the hearty chunks to the smooth pâté and airy mousse. This can help you better understand what your pet will be enjoying! You can watch it here!

Why Pets (and Pet Parents) Love Wet Food

  • Supports Hydration: This is perhaps the biggest advantage, especially crucial for cats. Cats naturally have a lower thirst drive than dogs, often not drinking enough water on their own. Wet food helps them get a significant portion of their daily water intake through their meals, which is vital for kidney and urinary tract health. For dogs, especially those who aren’t big drinkers, it’s also a great way to boost hydration.
  • Highly Palatable: Let’s be honest, wet food often smells and tastes more appealing to pets. The strong aroma and soft, varied textures (think smooth pâté, chunky stews, or mousse) can be a huge hit, especially with picky eaters, senior pets with a reduced sense of smell, or those recovering from illness. It’s like a gourmet meal for them!
  • Gentle on Teeth & Gums: For senior pets, puppies or kittens with developing teeth, or any pet with dental issues (like missing teeth or gum sensitivity), the soft consistency of wet food is much easier to chew and swallow compared to hard kibble. This can make mealtime a comfortable and enjoyable experience.
  • Easier Digestion: The higher moisture content and softer texture can make wet food easier for some pets to digest. Think of it this way: softer food is less work for their digestive system to break down, and the extra moisture helps everything move smoothly through their gut. This can be particularly beneficial for pets with sensitive stomachs or certain gastrointestinal conditions.
  • Weight Management: While it might seem counterintuitive, wet food can sometimes aid in weight management. Because of its high-water content, it’s less calorie-dense by volume. This means your pet can eat a larger portion and feel fuller without consuming as many calories, which can be helpful for pets on a vet approved diet.

Things to Keep in Mind with Wet Food

  • Needs Refrigeration & Short Shelf Life: Once opened, wet food needs to be refrigerated promptly to prevent spoilage, typically lasting only 2-3 days. This means you need to be mindful of portioning and storage.
  • Can Be Messier: Serving wet food can sometimes be a bit messier than dry kibble, and it might require more frequent cleaning of bowls.
  • No Dental Cleaning Benefits: Unlike some dry kibbles, wet food offers no abrasive action to help clean teeth. This means you’ll need to rely on other methods for dental hygiene, such as regular brushing or dental chews.

What is Dry Food (Kibble)?

Dry food, commonly known as kibble, consists of small, hard, crunchy pieces. It’s made by mixing ingredients, cooking them at high temperatures, and then shaping them into various shapes and sizes before drying. Kibble is low in moisture (typically 5-10%) and is the most widely used type of pet food due to its convenience and affordability.

Why Pet Parents (and Some Pets) Love Dry Food

Dry food’s widespread popularity largely comes down to several key factors that make pet parenting a little easier:

  • Convenience & Easy to Serve: Imagine just scooping food from a bag and pouring it into a bowl – that’s the ease of dry kibble! It’s less messy and a convenient option for busy schedules, making daily feeding quick and simple.
  • Affordability & Cost-Effective: Generally speaking, dry kibble offers more servings per bag compared to wet food, making it more cost-effective over time. This can be a significant advantage, especially for larger pets or households with multiple furry friends, helping you manage your budget without compromising on providing a complete diet.
  • Long Shelf Life & Convenient for Free-Feeding: Dry kibble has a significantly longer shelf life than wet food, even after the bag is opened, as long as it’s stored properly in an airtight container. This makes it ideal for bulk purchases, reducing how often you need to shop. It’s also great for “free-feeding“, which means you can leave it out throughout the day for your pet to graze on whenever they feel hungry, without worrying about spoilage. This offers great flexibility for both you and your pet, especially if your pet is good at managing their own food intake.
  • Dental Support (Limited): The crunchy texture of kibble can provide some cleaning action against your pet’s teeth as they chew, which may help to reduce plaque and tartar buildup in some cases. However, it’s crucial to understand that kibble alone is not a substitute for regular professional dental care or dedicated dental hygiene practices like brushing. Think of it as a minor aid, not a solution.

Things to Keep in Mind with Dry Food

  • Low Moisture Content: This is the primary concern, particularly for cats who often don’t drink enough water. A diet solely of dry food might contribute to chronic dehydration, which can impact kidney and urinary tract health over time. For dogs, while less critical, ensuring adequate water intake is still important. To help your pet stay well-hydrated, always ensure they have access to fresh, clean water, and consider using water fountains or adding water to their kibble.
  • Less Appealing to Some Pets: The texture and aroma of dry food can be less enticing to picky eaters compared to the rich smells and soft textures of wet food. For pets, especially cats with their keen sense of smell, a less aromatic and harder-to-chew food might simply not spark their interest as much.
  • Hard Texture Challenges: For older pets, puppies or kittens with sensitive gums, or pets with existing dental problems, the hard texture of kibble can be difficult or even painful to chew. In some rare cases, very hard kibble can contribute to dental wear or gum irritation over a long period.

Making the Choice: Wet or Dry?

So, with all this information, how do you decide what’s best for your furry companion? The ideal choice truly hinges on your pet’s individual needs, lifestyle, and health profile.

Wet food may be a better option if:

  • Your pet needs extra hydration: This is especially true for cats, who are prone to urinary tract issues if chronically dehydrated. Wet food significantly boosts their water intake.
  • They’re older, have missing teeth, or trouble chewing: The soft texture is much more comfortable for sensitive mouths.
  • They’re a fussy eater or have a reduced appetite: The strong aroma and appealing texture can entice even the most reluctant eaters.
  • They have urinary or kidney concerns (especially cats): Increased water intake from wet food can help dilute urine and support kidney function.
  • You’re looking for lower-calorie food option by volume: This can be beneficial for pets needing to lose weight, as they can eat a more satisfying portion for fewer calories.

Dry food may be a better option if:

  • You’re looking for a convenient, budget-friendly option: Its long shelf life and ease of serving make it a practical choice for many households.
  • Your pet drinks enough water on their own: If your dog or cat consistently drinks plenty of fresh water throughout the day, the lower moisture content of kibble might not be a significant concern.
  • You want some support for dental health: While not a substitute for professional care, the crunch can contribute to cleaner teeth.
  • You’re managing weight or portion control with specific feeding guidelines: Dry food allows for precise portioning, which is crucial for managing your pet’s weight.
  • You prefer free-feeding: If your pet self-regulates their intake well, leaving kibble out can be very convenient.

The Best of Both Worlds: Combination Feeding

Many pet parents find that the perfect solution isn’t one or the other, but a clever combination! Combination feeding involves mixing both wet and dry food, allowing your pet to enjoy the benefits of both.

Why Mix It Up? The Advantages of Combination Feeding

  • Enhanced Hydration: By adding wet food to their diet, you boost your pet’s overall water intake. This helps dilute their urine, reducing the risk of common issues like urinary crystals and stones. Adequate hydration is essential for maintaining healthy kidney function and supporting overall organ health.
  • Variety in Taste and Texture: Pets, like humans, appreciate variety! Mixing textures and flavours can make mealtime more exciting and prevent boredom with their diet. Think about it: eating the same meal, day in and day out, can get pretty dull, even for our furry friends! Pets, especially those with a strong sense of smell and taste, can experience ‘food fatigue’ if their diet lacks variation. Offering different textures (crunchy kibble, soft pâté) and flavours can stimulate their senses and keep them engaged at mealtime, preventing them from becoming uninterested or picky.
  • Increased Appetite Appeal: For picky eaters, adding a spoonful of highly palatable wet food to their dry kibble can make the entire meal much more enticing. The strong aroma and rich flavours of wet food act as a powerful attractant, stimulating your pet’s appetite and encouraging them to eat their entire meal. This is especially helpful for pets who are recovering from illness, have a reduced sense of smell due to age, or simply need a little extra encouragement to finish their food.
  • Nutritional Flexibility: You can strategically combine different formulas to meet specific nutritional goals. For example, a senior formula kibble with a hydrating wet food, or a weight management kibble with a low-calorie wet food. This approach allows you to fine-tune your pet’s diet precisely, and the flexibility ensures your pet gets exactly what they need, addressing multiple health or life stage requirements simultaneously.

How to Practice Combination Feeding Safely

When combining foods, it’s crucial to adjust portion sizes to avoid overfeeding and prevent weight gain. Since you’re now feeding two types of food, the total daily calorie intake must be carefully managed to ensure your pet gets balanced nutrition. The best approach is to reduce the amount of dry food when adding wet food. Always follow the feeding guidelines on both product labels.

To ensure a smooth transition and maintain a healthy weight, here are the most important pointers:

  • Substitute, Don’t Just Add: If you simply add wet food on top of your pet’s regular kibble, you will likely overfeed them. The goal is to replace a portion of the kibble with wet food.
  • Measure Accurately: Use a standard measuring cup or kitchen scale to precisely measure portions. This prevents accidental overfeeding.
  • Transition Slowly: Introduce the wet food gradually over 7-10 days by mixing a small amount with the dry food.
  • Monitor Weight: Keep a close eye on your pet’s body condition. If you notice weight gain, adjust their daily portions accordingly.

Practical Examples

Cat: For “Milo,” an indoor cat who eats a half-cup of dry kibble per day, add an 100g pouch of wet food by mixing the wet food with a quarter-cup of kibble in a single serving. This keeps his total calorie intake balanced.

Dog: For “Buddy,” a 30Kg adult Labrador, who eats three cups of dry food daily, mix a half-can of wet food into his meal. To balance this, you would reduce his dry food intake to two cups per day, split between his meals.

Beyond the Bowl: Key Considerations for Every Pet Parent

Choosing the right food is more than just picking wet or dry; it’s about understanding your pet’s entire dietary picture.

  • Age and Size: Puppies and kittens have different nutritional needs than adult or senior pets. Their food should be specifically formulated for their life stage to support proper growth and development. Small breeds might need smaller kibble, while large breeds have specific joint support requirements. It’s important to know their life stage because their bodies are constantly changing and require different levels of nutrients and energy. For example:
  • Puppies and Kittens: These little ones are growing rapidly! They need significantly more calories, protein, and specific vitamins and minerals (like calcium and phosphorus for bone development) to fuel their intense growth, build strong muscles, and develop their immune systems. Feeding them adult food would mean they miss out on these crucial building blocks, potentially leading to developmental issues.
  • Adult Pets: Once growth is complete, their energy needs stabilize. Adult food is formulated to maintain their health, weight, and energy levels without promoting excessive growth or weight gain.
  • Senior Pets: As pets age, their metabolism slows down, and they might become less active. They may also develop age-related conditions like arthritis or kidney issues. Senior formulas often have fewer calories to prevent weight gain, and may include ingredients to support joint health, kidney function, or cognitive well-being.
  • Breed-Specific Needs: Beyond age, a small breed puppy needs tiny kibble they can easily chew, while a large breed puppy needs carefully controlled calcium and phosphorus levels to prevent rapid growth that could harm their developing joints.
  • Breed and Activity Level: A highly active working dog will require a much more calorie-dense diet than a sedentary lap cat. Breed-specific foods can sometimes cater to unique needs, though individual assessment is always key. Think of it like this: an energetic Border Collie herding sheep all day burns far more calories than a quiet Persian cat lounging on the sofa! Their metabolism and energy output are vastly different.

Activity Level

  • Active Pets: Energetic dogs and cats, such as a working Labrador or a playful Bengal, have a higher metabolism and need food with higher calories, protein, and fat to fuel their energy and maintain muscle mass.
  • Less Active Pets: Older pets or those with a relaxed lifestyle need fewer calories. Managing their weight is critical to prevent serious issues like joint problems, diabetes, and heart disease.

Breed-Specific Needs

Some breeds have health predispositions that a specialized diet can help manage.

  • Dogs: Large breed dogs are often prone to joint problems, so their food might include ingredients like glucosamine and chondroitin for joint support. Other formulas can address common issues like skin sensitivities, digestive problems, or feature specially shaped kibble to suit the unique jaw structures of breeds like Bulldogs, Pugs, and Boxers.
  • Cats: Some breed-specific foods might target hairball reduction for long-haired breeds or support urinary health for breeds prone to such issues.

 

  • Health Conditions or Special Dietary Needs: This is paramount. Pets with allergies, sensitive stomachs, kidney disease, diabetes, or dental issues will require specific dietary considerations. Just like humans with specific health needs, pets often benefit immensely from specialized diets. For example:

 

  • Allergies/Sensitivities: If your pet has food allergies (which can cause skin itching, ear infections, or digestive upset), they might need a “limited ingredient” diet or a “hydrolysed protein” diet where proteins are broken down into tiny pieces to avoid triggering an immune response.
  • Kidney Disease: Pets with kidney issues often require diets lower in protein, phosphorus, and sodium, but with higher levels of certain B vitamins and omega-3 fatty acids to support kidney function and reduce the workload on these organs.
  • Diabetes: Diabetic pets need diets that help stabilize blood sugar levels, often with controlled carbohydrate content and high fibre.
  • Dental Issues: Beyond just the texture of food, some specific dental diets are designed with unique kibble shapes and textures that physically scrub the teeth, or contain ingredients that help reduce plaque and tartar formation.
  • Digestive Upset: For pets with sensitive stomachs, diets with easily digestible ingredients, prebiotics, and probiotics can help soothe their digestive system and promote a healthy gut microbiome.
  • Weight Management: For pets needing to lose or gain weight, specialized formulas exist that are either lower in calories and higher in fibre (for weight loss) or more calorie-dense (for weight gain), ensuring they reach a healthy body condition.

These specialized diets are not just “different” foods; they are scientifically formulated to help manage specific medical conditions and improve your pet’s quality of life. Always work closely with your veterinarian to determine if your pet needs a prescription diet or a specially formulated therapeutic food.

  • Taste Preferences: At the end of the day, your pet has to eat the food! If they consistently refuse to eat, they won’t get the necessary nutrients, leading to weight loss and a decline in health. Finding a food that your pet genuinely looks forward to is vital for their overall well-being and ensures consistent, balanced nutrition.
  • Reading Food Labels: Don’t just look at the front of the bag! Turn it over and read the ingredients list. Ingredients are listed by weight, so the first few ingredients make up the bulk of the food. Look for whole protein sources (like chicken, lamb, salmon) as primary ingredients. Understanding terms like “AAFCO Statement” is also important – this indicates the food meets minimum nutritional requirements for a specific life stage.

What is AAFCO and ISO Certification?

Choosing the right food is easier when you understand what the labels mean. At PetStore Kenya, we’re proud to offer products that meet the highest standards.

  • AAFCO Certification: The Association of American Feed Control Officials (AAFCO) is a non-profit that sets the nutritional standards for pet food. An AAFCO statement on a label is a guarantee that the food provides a complete and balanced diet for your pet’s specific life stage, according to expert guidelines. It’s your assurance of a nutritionally sound diet.
  • ISO Certification: The International Organization for Standardization (ISO) sets global benchmarks for quality and consistency in manufacturing. An ISO certification means the food is produced under rigorous international standards, with a strong focus on quality control, safety, and reliability. It’s an added layer of assurance for the consistency and safety of every bag.

PetStore Kenya’s Double Certification: Our pet food is both AAFCO and ISO certified, giving you peace of mind that you’re choosing a product that is not only nutritionally complete but also produced with the highest quality and safety standards.

For best results, always consult your veterinarian before making big changes to your pet’s diet, especially if they have health concerns like kidney disease, diabetes, or dental issues. Your vet can provide personalized advice tailored to your pet’s unique characteristics and health status.

Your Partner in Pet Nutrition: PetStore Kenya 🛒

At PetStore Kenya, we understand that choosing the right food can feel overwhelming. That’s why we’re committed to being your trusted partner in pet nutrition. We offer a wide selection of both wet and dry food tailored to your pet’s needs from sterilised and senior formulas to low-grain and breed-specific options. Whether you’re feeding a fussy cat, a growing puppy, or a senior dog with sensitive teeth, we have something for every bowl!

We encourage you to explore our diverse range of high-quality pet foods. Our team is always ready to provide personalized nutrition advice, helping you navigate the choices and find the perfect fit for your furry friend.

👉 Browse our pet food selection here

📍 Visit us in-store at one of our convenient locations for personalized advice

💬 Chat with our team online via WhatsApp for quick questions and tips. You can also join our WhatsApp Channel for more tips and updates.

Conclusion: Nourishing Happy, Healthy Pets Every Day

Choosing between wet and dry food, or finding the perfect combination, is a significant decision for your pet’s health. By understanding the unique benefits and considerations of each, and by focusing on your pet’s individual needs, you’re already on the path to making the best possible choice.

Remember, a well-nourished pet is a happy pet, and a happy pet makes for a happy home. We are here to support you every step of the way in nourishing happy, healthy pets every day!

The PetStore Kenya Team

We ❤️ Them As Much As You Do!

Just like humans, a balanced diet is the Read More

title: How does your cat food compare?
content:
October 25th 2022:

View/Download Cat Food Comparison Chart 2020

We understand that there are small variances among brands of cat food. For this comparison chart, the following specific cat food products were used and the information was taken from the manufacturer’s website or product packaging:

  • Royal Canin: Adult Fit32
  • Spectrum: Adult Delicate32
  • Reflex Plus: Adult Hairball
  • Reflex: Adult Chicken
  • Bonnie: Adult Cocktail
  • Proline: Adult Fish
  • King: Adult Fish
  • Lara: Adult Beef
  • Snappy Tom: Adult Ocean fish with vegetables
  • Josi Cat: Adult Poultry
  • Bewi Cat: Adult Poultry

 

Grading Criteria

Animal Protein as First Ingredient

International labeling standards state that the list of ingredients must be in the order of proportion. Having animal protein as the first ingredient means that the highest proportion ingredient in the food is in fact animal protein. Cats are obligate carnivores so animal protein is the most important component of a cat’s diet

Multiple Animal-Based Proteins

Certain cats prefer certain protein sources, once you find a brand your cat likes, it’s good to have options of protein sources

Greater than 30% protein

Protein is the most important and one of the more expensive ingredients in dry cat food. The higher the protein the better. There are certain health conditions whereby a lower protein content is better – consult a vet for more information

Omega 3 & 6 Fats

Omega 3 & 6 fatty acids are important for the cat’s eyes, skin and nails, these fats can be derived from either flaxseed oil or fish oils.

Animal-based Omega 3 & 6 Fats

Omega 3 & 6 fats from animal sources are more bioavailable than from vegetable sources. I.e. The cat can absorb more of these essential oils. Oils from fish fat are better than flaxseed. The most superior source of omega 3 & 6 is actually Krill Oil (or Qrill oil)

Prebiotics and Probiotics

In order to ensure proper digestion, good gut bacteria is essential (probiotics). These good bacteria need a source of food (prebiotics) and these usually come in the form of indigestible fibers otherwise known as polysaccharides. Examples include fructooligosaccharides and mannanoligosaccharides

Immune Boosters

Immune boosters ensure that your cat stays healthy. The most common immune boosters in dry cat food are brewer’s yeast and beta-glucan

Dehydrated / Hydrolyzed Protein

Dehydrated protein means actual mean that is simply dehydrated then compressed into a kibble – yes! actual non processed meat. Hydrolyzed proteins are proteins that are broken down into amino acids and peptides – these are the most absorbable type of protein molecule and are particularly useful when dealing with food intolerances or protein intolerances

Gluten Free Option

Some cats have a gluten intolerance. A gluten-free option is useful for cats with this issue.

Botanicals

Botanicals are added to dry food to boost the cat’s health and vitality in various ways. Examples include citrus bioflavonoids for extra antioxidant protection, curcumin for anti-inflammation and joint health and rosemary for keeping bugs away

No Animal Derivatives or By-Products

Animal derivatives and by-products are parts of the animal carcass that are not actual meat, e.g. skin, blood, fur, etc. anything that can be classified as a protein. These products are generally regarded as less preferable for animals to eat compared to actual meat or organs.

For this comparison chart, the following specific cat food products were used and the information was taken from the manufacturer’s website or product packaging: Royal Canin: Adult Read More

title: What is Aflatoxin? – Pet Food Safety of PetStore Kenya Brands
content:
Nairobi, KENYA, May 13th 2020: Petstore Kenya and its affiliate Loki Ventures Ltd have been made aware of a number of issues related to possible aflatoxin contamination of ready to eat dog food (kibble) in the market.

What is aflatoxin?

Aflatoxins are a family of toxins produced by certain fungi are found on agricultural crops.

How does a pet get aflatoxin?

Most dry pet food has a vegetable, grain or animal component. Either the grain itself may be contaminated or the animal component (or both) may be contaminated at the start of the manufacturing process.

There is a risk of aflatoxin contamination reaching your pet IF a raw material (like those mentioned above) has not been tested properly for aflatoxin and is processed into a dry kibble AND the kibble is not later tested for aflatoxin.

How can I be sure my pet food is safe?

Send a batch to any of the independent labs for aflatoxin testing: Nairobi University Vet lab, Analabs, Prolab, Crop Nuts, etc.

We would like to ensure our customers and stakeholders that Loki Ventures Ltd and PetStore Kenya only import and distribute dog food and cat food of the highest possible standard: King, Trendline, Bonnie, Proline, Reflex and Spectrum.

We work exclusively with a factory in Turkey that is compliant with the following international certifications and standards of manufacturing:

  • ISO 9001:2008
  • ISO 22000:2005
  • CE – conformity to EU standards

We selected this factory as they align with our company values around “healthy, happy pets”. Our Directors have verified, in person, the following good manufacturing practices:

  1. Every batch of raw material that is used in kibble production is examined via spectrometry for compliance.
  2. Every batch of agricultural input is tested for aflatoxin.
  3. Every batch of produced food is tested for conformity and contaminants, including aflatoxin.
  4. Aflatoxin blockers are incorporated into the food itself so that the risk of aflatoxin contamination is
    minimized in the event of an unlikely exposure.

CLICK HERE to learn more about quality management and pet food safety.

Once in our warehouse, all pet food is stored in a well-ventilated, cool warehouse out of any direct exposure to sunlight or humidity thereby bringing any risk of aflatoxin from storage down to nil.

When switching cat food or dog food, always remember to follow the 7-day transition protocol stated on the packaging. Take note that diarrhea and an upset stomach for up to 10 days is a normal occurrence while new gut flora colonise.

We thank you for your continued patronage. We would be happy to answer any further questions. Please CLICK HERE to contact us.

title: How to Evaluate Which Food Is Best for Your Dog:  A Comprehensive Guide
content:
November 29th 2022:
When it comes to feeding your dog, you want to make sure you are giving them the best possible food for their individual needs. But with all the different options, how can you know which one is right for your pup?

 

This comprehensive guide will go over how to evaluate which food is best for your dog.

 

Consider Your Dog’s Age

 

Consider your dog’s age when choosing his food . Puppies, for example, have different nutritional needs than adult dogs. They need more calories, easy to digest protein and minerals to support their growth and development and certain vitamins and minerals for their growing bones and teeth. Senior dogs, on the other hand, may need a different formula that is easier to digest and has fewer calories to help them maintain a healthy weight. There are also special formulas for pregnant and nursing dogs that provide the extra nutrients they need during this time.

“Keep Their Food Fresh”: Easy Tips on Storing Dog and Cat Food”Keep Their Food Fresh”: Easy Tips on Storing Dog and Cat Food

Does your dog have allergies?

The market is flooded with different types of dog food, yet not all of them will not be suitable for your dog. If your dog has allergies, you will need to find food that does not contain any of the allergens that they are allergic to.

 

Dogs with allergies may need hypoallergenic food. A dog can also be intolerant to a specific protein source, so look out for formulations that are “mono-protien” ie are made with one protein, this allows for easier diagnosis of the problematic protein source.

 

Your veterinarian can help you choose a food that is right for your dog’s individual needs.

 

Consider Your Dog’s Activity Level

 

Another factor to consider when choosing food for your dog is their activity level. Active dogs, such as those who participate in sports or have high energy, will need food that will help them maintain their energy levels. therefore, they need food that is higher in calories and protein . Less active dogs, on the other hand, may need food that is lower in calories to help them avoid weight gain.

 

Read and compare the dog food labels

.

Reading the food label sounds like an obvious way to distinguish good food from bad food, but it is easier said than done. Dog food labels can be hard to read, both due to the small print and the unspoken awkwardness of handling big bags of dog food in the store!

 

Take some time to look at the list of all the ingredients that make up the product.

 

Whenever possible, choose a source of high-quality animal protein. Dogs thrive on animal protein.

 

Conclusion:

 

Ultimately, the best dog food for your pup is up to you to decide. You’re the one who sees your pup regularly.

 

If they produce firm, healthy stool, are active and fit, and have a healthy appetite, their dog food is probably working fine. However, if the reverse is true, you should consider switching to a different pet food brand.

 

During this process, your veterinarian can be a valuable resource to assist you in knowing more about pet nutrition.
excerpt: November 29th 2022: When it comes to feeding your dog, you want to make sure you are giving them the best possible food for their individual needs. But with all the different options, how can you know which one is right for your pup?   This comprehensive guide will go over how to evaluate which Read More

title: "Keep Their Food Fresh": Easy Tips on Storing Dog and Cat Food
content:
November 22nd 2022:
Have  you ever had your fur baby experience an unexplained bout of loose stool or vomiting ?

 

You may want to check your pet food storage methods.

 

Poor pet food storage can be the cause of these issues, as bacteria or spoilage can occur if we’re not being careful how and where we store our pet’s food.

 

We all love our fur babies, and as pet parents, we want to make sure that they are well-fed and healthy. With that comes the need for the correct pet food storage.

 

This article is for busy paw parents who need fast and easy tips to properly store their pet food so that it stays fresh and nutritious for your fur buddy as well as

to avoid spoilage and wastage..

 

How do I correctly Store Dry Pet Food?

The rule of thumb with storing dry pet food is to ensure that it is  stored in a cool, dry place away from sunlight and heat.

 

Dry pet food storage tips

  • It is best to make sure the food is stored in its original packaging.

When you need to decant into an airtight container, do not mix it with  different bags of pet food. This is because you might not know how long some of the food has been sitting there.

  • Keep the original packaging of the pet food as you might need it for reference to check the expiration dates of dry pet food to ensure your pet stays healthy.
  • Storage bags should always be sealed. The top of the bag can be rolled and closed with a clip if the bag cannot be resealed.

 

Wet pet food storage tips

  • Wet food should be stored in a cool, dry place
  • Refrigerate open wet food. Store remaining food in an airtight container if you haven’t used it all. You can use a plastic container with an airtight lid, to store it in the fridge.
  • Wet food should not be left out  in the open for more than 4 hours.

 

In conclusion we have established that properly storing pet food is important to keep your furry friend healthy and safe. By following these simple tips, you can ensure that your pet’s food will stay fresh and delicious.

title: Why is my Cat not eating? Hunger Strike or Anorexia in cats
content:
November 15th 2022:
Did you know that cats also suffer from anorexia?

Yes, our feline friends are known to be fussy eaters but unfortunately, that’s not always the case.

 

If your cat used to love their food but now turns their nose up at it, it could be a sign that something is wrong and you might need to look out for the symptoms that are highlighted as you read along, to know if your cat might be suffering from anorexia.

 

Cat anorexia is a condition characterised by a lack of interest in food and weight loss.

 

While the exact cause of cat anorexia is unknown, there are a number of potential contributing factors, including stress, changes in routine or in the home environment, and underlying health conditions.

 

There are two types of cat anorexia:

 

Types

True anorexia

True Anorexia refers to a cat that does not want to eat at all.

 

Pseudo-anorexia

It occurs when a cat is interested in food but cannot due to complications such as  inflammation,tumours, disease, and pain which causes the cat to have difficulty chewing or grabbing food.

 

Symptoms of cat anorexia include

 

  • Complete loss of appetite
  • Weakness
  • Weight loss
  • Icterus (yellowing of the skin)
  • Lethargy
  • Excesses salivation
  • Diarrhoea
  • Vomiting

 

If your cat is showing any of these symptoms, it is important to take them to the vet for a check-up.

 

Treatment for cat anorexia will vary depending on the underlying cause. If your cat is ill, treatment will focus on addressing the underlying condition. If stress is the cause, you may need to make changes to your cat’s environment to help them feel more comfortable. If your cat is simply not interested in food, you may need to try a different type of food or change their feeding schedule.

 

With proper treatment, most cats make a full recovery and return to their normal weight and activity level.

Yes, our feline friends are known to be fussy eaters but unfortunately, that’s not always the case.   If your cat used to love their food but now turns their nose up at it, it could be a sign that something is wrong Read More

title: Do You Know Why Your Cat Sleeps So Much? 4 Reasons Your Cat May Be Sleeping All Day
content:
November 8th 2022:
Did you know that cats can sleep an average of 15 hours daily?

Some stretch that toward 20 hours, especially for older cats and kittens.

 

We see so many cute and funny videos on our phones of how much cats love sleeping all day, every day, literally anywhere and everywhere. Behind the curtain, on the couch, at the kitchen counter, on your bed, on your computer, on your printer, on your head, if you’re a cat parent, you get the gist.

 

Have you ever asked yourself why this is the case?

It turns out there are a few reasons why cats may be sleeping all day.

 

  1. To Conserve Energy

 

Cats are predators. Like many Predators, they conserve energy by resting or sleeping, saving up for periods when they will do their best hunting.

Sleep helps cats conserve energy for hunting activities such as stalking, chasing, pouncing, climbing, and running.

 

In the wild, cats spend a lot of time stalking and hunting their prey. This can be quite energy-intensive, so they often take naps between hunts to rest and recharge.

 

While your domestic cat probably isn’t hunting for their next meal, they still have that instinctual predatory drive. As a result, they may spend a lot of time sleeping to conserve their energy.

 

  1. Cats are crepuscular creatures

    .

Cats being crepuscular creatures is another reason they may be sleeping a lot. Crepuscular means that they are mostly active at dawn and dusk.

 

 

Since your cat is probably indoors most of the time, they may not be getting the natural light cues that would tell them when it’s time to be active. As a result, they may sleep all day since it’s their natural inclination to do so.

 

  1. Your cat may be sick or uncomfortable

    .

 

Contrary to popular belief, oversleeping can be a sign of a medical issue. When cats are injured or sick, they often sleep more to help their bodies recover.

 

The extra sleep gives their bodies the time and energy they need to repair any damage and fight off any infection. So if your cat has been sleeping a lot lately, it may be because they’re not feeling well, especially if she is no longer engaging in pleasurable activities like playtime, feeding, and petting.

 

Also, If your cat is overweight, she may have trouble moving around much, which leads to her lying down often. Obesity in cats is a serious problem that warrants an appointment with your veterinarian, who will have her overall health assessed and prescribe healthy pet food that will aid in weight loss.

 

  1. Your Cat Might Be Bored

Sometimes cats sleep more when there is nothing to do. To minimise boredom, you can enrich her life in many ways.

 

One of the ways you can do this is by keeping her entertained and busy by providing interactive pet toys specialised for cats.

 

Ensuring you are also actively involved in playtime is an excellent way to strengthen your bond with your feline friend. Play together and keep her active, especially with toys or activities that use her hunting skills.

 

The good news is, if sleeping were an Olympic sport, your cat would probably win a gold medal; however, If you’re concerned about your cat’s sleeping habits, talk to your vet.

Behind Read More

title: Pet Obesity: A Growing Problem. How to help your furry friend reach an optimal weight.
content:
November 1st 2022:
Pet obesity is a growing problem worldwide. At PetStore Kenya, we genuinely care that your pets are kept well and healthy. We hope to tackle this topic to help pet owners who currently have pets struggling with obesity or shed light for those who are not aware that their pet may be overweight.

How do I identify if my pet is obese or overweight?

Half the challenge is to recognise if your pet is overweight. Like humans, dogs and cats come in all shapes and sizes. The best way to determine obesity is to use the Body Condition Score System.

The Body Condition Score System focuses on the amount of skin and tissue that can be felt over the ribs, as well as the shape over the waist and underbelly. A dog or cat with an ideal body condition will have a visible waist behind the ribs when viewed from above; the ribs can be felt through only a small amount of fat; and, when viewed from the side, the underbelly is tucked up.

Your veterinarian can provide guidance in determining an ideal body weight for your pet based on breed, age and activity level. Use the infographic below to help you determine whether your pet is in the ideal shape. Contact your veterinarian if you are not sure and / or want a consultation to determine your pet’s current Body Condition Score.

What’s the big deal? What problems can obesity cause?

If your dog or cat is obese, chances are that she suffers from chronic pain due to the excess weight they carry. Weight loss is the safest and most effective way to alleviate chronic pain.

Overweight dogs are more likely to suffer from joint disease, respiratory disease and other preventable conditions. Overweight cats are at risk for diabetes and some types of liver disease. These conditions can have devastating consequences and become financially challenging for pet owners. Managing your pet’s weight is important to their overall health and lifelong wellness.

Below are guiding images to help identify whether your pet is beyond his ideal body condition.

How do I help my pet reach an optimal weight?

Helping your pudgy pup or cat shed weight can be challenging. But success is extremely rewarding when your pet’s pain decreases, energy increases and fitness level improves. A successful weight loss programme addresses two major components: diet (amount and type of food) and current activity level to help burn off calories.

At PetStore Kenya, we bring you solutions for both diet and activity level.

Our functional line, Spectrum, offers Light27 for dogs and Slim34 for cats. Both formulas limit weight gain by using sugar cane fibre to regulate food intake. The fibre helps them feel full without extra calories. And don’t forget that treats have calories too. Remember to include the calories from treats in your pet’s daily calorie intake.

We offer a range of outdoor toys that will naturally entice your pet to increase his activity level and have fun at the same time!


Visit our website for all products mentioned and shown here. Your pet’s weight loss package of food and toys is just a few clicks away. Start your pet’s successful weight loss programme today! And remember, if your pet is unsatisfied with any of our products, we offer a 100% guaranteed refund.

title: Coronavirus (COVID-19) Statement
content:
With the coronavirus (COVID-19) situation developing quickly, we would like to take a moment to share with our community how PetStore Kenya is responding to the outbreak. We are committed to caring for the health and well-being of our Team, partners, human customers and their furry best friends.

Hygiene is key. We are adhering to the advice and guidelines provided by the World Health Organisation (WHO) and adapting our operational practices accordingly. We are taking the following precautions and preventative measures:

  • Our warehouse Team is screened daily for any signs of illness and adhere to a strict schedule of hand and glove -sanitising whilst handling all products.
  • All of our delivery drivers will wear gloves and sanitise their gloves and hands before and after deliveries and wear N-95 specification masks.
  • You have the option for no-contact delivery service – you may request in your order notes that your delivery should be left on your doorstep.

If you find that your local supermarket is out-of-stock on a particular item, we encourage you to [order directly from our online store](insert UTM link). Our warehouse is stocked and our Team is ready to fulfill your order as quickly as possible.

As you know, we endeavour to provide excellent customer service – as this is what makes us different. Our Team continues to work tirelessly to provide you with the very best experience. We appreciate your patience with us during these uncertain times.

Thank you for your support and wish you and your families well.
